tokens.text: Sawmill
Produces {0} Wood daily.
Wood is the bedrock of survival. From construction and fuel to crafting of arms, its utility is unmatched.
Ore Mine
Produces {0} Ore daily.
The ores of Jadame abound with a diverse array of metals.
Gold Mine
Produces {0} Gold daily.
Gold is not only the most important medium of exchange but also a popular magical component.
Gem Mound
Produces {0} Gems daily.
The seductive gleam of precious gems has sown much strife.
Crystal Vein
Produces {0} Crystals daily.
Experienced alchemists know it is not the crystal that matters, but its contents.
Mercury Fissure
Produces {0} Mercury daily.
A unique substance, perfectly suited for arcane rites.
Alchemical Lab
Allows Gems, Crystals and Mercury to be converted into Alchemical Dust.
A haven for alchemists seeking the perfect formula, where each experiment brings them closer to their grand masterpiece.
Wood Storage
Once per week, gives {0} Wood to the first visiting hero.
An abandoned woodpile. If it isn’t broken down, the wood will soon begin to rot.
Ore Storage
Once per week, gives {0} Ore to the first visiting hero.
The storehouse is filled to the brim with ore, yet not a miner or guard is to be found.
Gold Storage
Once per week, gives {0} Gold to the first visiting hero.
Whoever left it here, you absolutely need it more.
Gem Storage
Once per week, gives {0} Gems to the first visiting hero.
Who could have left so many gems in the heart of the wilderness? It is unlikely you will ever know.
Crystal Storage
Once per week, gives {0} Crystals to the first visiting hero.
Near this storage, you feel a faint tremor and a strange surge of power.
Mercury Storage
Once per week, gives {0} Mercury to the first visiting hero.
Mercury vapors emanating from this place permeate the surroundings, causing insomnia and headaches among the locals.
Alchemical Dust Storage
Once per week, gives {0} Alchemical Dust to the first visiting hero.
Alchemical dust is stored here, mingled with herbs and tools that are useless to you.
Treasure Chest
Gives a choice of either {0} Gold or half that amount in XP.
A choice between fleeting riches and timeless wisdom.
Gold
Gives {0} Gold.
Everybody likes Gold. Even demons and spirits.
Wood
Gives {0} Wood.
Logs, planks, beams, and woodchips.
Ore
Gives {0} Ore.
Value lies both in the raw rock and what can be extracted from it.
Gems
Gives {0} Gems.
How many lives have been claimed because of these jewels?..
Crystals
Gives {0} Crystals.
Some shine differently than the rest.
Mercury
Gives {0} Mercury.
The only metal that defies the laws of reason. Perfect for esoteric rites.
Alchemical Dust
Gives {0} Alchemical Dust.
Essential for upgrading artifacts and more.
Pandora’s Box
Gives an unpredictable but great reward.
Did you know it was actually a large jar, not a box?
Campfire
Gives a small amount of random resources.
Whatever it was before, it’s nothing but ash and base elements now.
Magic Garden
Once per week, gives Gold or valuable resources to the first visiting hero.
Magic garden of magic riches.
Village
Once per week, gives the first visiting hero a common resource of their choice.
Some call it racketeering; politicians call it taxes.
Gingerbread House
Once per week, gives the first visiting hero a valuable resource of their choice.
The wicked witch will pay you well to ignore her cellar.
Windmill
Once per week, gives the first visiting hero a small amount of random resources.
The miller will pay his sovereign, but he doesn’t have anything fancy.
Trophy Hunter’s Den
Once per week, gives the first visiting hero a random reward.
When he drinks, he gives away his trophies freely. And he drinks often.
Lost Library
Gives the first visiting hero {0} XP.
The steps run endless — to the very Abyss, they say.
Tear of Truth
Once per week for each hero, fully restores the hero’s Movement points.
Take a sip of this dew and feel a surge of newfound vigor.
Stinging Sword
Permanently increases each visiting hero’s Attack by {0}.
Increases each visiting hero’s Attack by {0} until the end of the mission.
What hero of old left it behind… and why?
Summit Automaton
Permanently increases each visiting hero’s Defense by {0}.
Increases each visiting hero’s Defense by {0} until the end of the mission.
The automated smith repairs any armor placed before it.
Magic Wheel
Permanently increases each visiting hero’s Spell Power by {0}.
Increases each visiting hero’s Spell Power by {0} until the end of the mission.
Meditating before the wheel mysteriously imbues you with magical power.
Knowledge Garden
Permanently increases each visiting hero’s Knowledge by {0}.
Increases each visiting hero’s Knowledge by {0} until the end of the mission.
The best place to read.
Scales of Worth
Permanently increases each visiting hero’s chosen attribute by {0}.
Increases each visiting hero’s chosen attribute by {0} until the end of the mission.
The “weight” of a heart rests upon the virtue of its bearer.
Living Maze
Permanently increases each visiting hero’s chosen attribute by {0}.
Increases each visiting hero’s chosen attribute by {0} until the end of the mission.
A labyrinth of self‑discovery, where only the strongest emerge with their true strength.
Pauper Knight Order
Permanently increases each visiting hero’s Attack or Defense by {0} for {1} Gold.
Increases each visiting hero’s Attack or Defense by {0} until the end of the mission for {1} Gold.
They swore off magic, compensating with relentless training.
Four Scholars Observatory
Permanently increases each visiting hero’s Spell Power or Knowledge by {0} for {1} Gold.
Increases each visiting hero’s Spell Power or Knowledge by {0} until the end of the mission for {1} Gold.
The Four Scholars wrote spells in the stars. Now it’s up to us to read them.
Mountain Monastery
Permanently increases each visiting hero’s Attack, Defense, Spell Power, and Knowledge by {0} for {1} Gold.
Increases each visiting hero’s Attack, Defense, Spell Power, and Knowledge by {0} until the end of the mission for {1} Gold.
These monasteries, built for enlightenment and serenity, hold generations of knowledge — accessible for a price.
World Mirror
Removes the fog of war in a small radius around each Mirror on the map.
The mirror’s vision of distant lands stirs both yearning and dread.
Quix’s Altar
Increases each visiting hero’s Morale by {0} until the end of the week.
The godling of aimless wanderlust bestows blessings upon those who pass through here.
Beer Fountain
Increases each visiting hero’s Luck by {0} until the end of the week.
Oftentimes called “liquid gold”, a good beer can spark many adventures and tales.
Pile of Books
Increases XP gained by each visiting hero by {0}% until the end of the week.
The bits and pieces you read here will linger in your mind for a while.
Whispering Stones
Increases each visiting hero’s maximum mana by {0}% until the end of the week.
A small tear in reality lets pure magic seep through. Use it!
Nightshade Shrine
Allows the hero to pick and learn {0} out of {1} random Nightshade spells.
Naira’s spells usually deal with curses, defense, and mental effects.
Daylight Shrine
Allows the hero to pick and learn {0} out of {1} random Daylight spells.
Arina’s spells usually deal with tactical advantages, positive effects, and healing.
Arcane Shrine
Allows the hero to pick and learn {0} out of {1} random Arcane spells.
Doreath’s spells usually deal with pure concepts, bending space and time.
Primal Shrine
Allows the hero to pick and learn {0} out of {1} random Primal spells.
Hksmilla’s spells are mostly powerful attacks with various effects.
Altar of Insight
Grants {0} Insight(s) to the first visiting hero. It can be used as usual to learn or upgrade Global Map spells.
This altar holds star-born knowledge, revealed only to those deemed worthy.
Endless Scroll of Gold
While controlled by a player, increases the level of all Daylight spells in the Observatory.
Written in golden ink, this endless living scroll holds an essential truth of magic.
Endless Scroll of Silver
While controlled by a player, increases the level of all Nightshade spells in the Observatory.
The scroll can be unrolled further and further, its secrets ever‑changing and never‑repeating.
Endless Scroll of Platinum
While controlled by a player, increases the level of all Arcane spells in the Observatory.
Reading the secrets of this scroll fills the soul with awe of the incomprehensible.
Endless Scroll of Bronze
While controlled by a player, increases the level of all Primal spells in the Observatory.
The scroll vibrates, and the words upon it quiver rhythmically, as if they were alive.
Dragon Step
Once per week for each hero, fully restores each visiting hero’s mana, then doubles it.
Olden dragons were so full of magic, even their footprints buzz with it.
Celestial Spire
Allows the study of all spells contained within the magical observatory.
The closer you are to the sky, the better you see the spells spelled out in the stars.
Four Scholars Shrine
Resets all skills and attributes gained from levels, allowing one to re‑train the hero. Only once per hero.
Arina, Naira, Doreath, and Hksmilla still walk Enroth. And by communing with them, heroes can receive their guidance, through which they might find new paths
Tree of Knowledge
Gives one level to each visiting hero.
Connected to the Murmurwood’s Mycelium, this tree knows every tale. But how do its brethren on other continents learn things?
Wise Owl
Teaches a random skill to each visiting hero. If they already know this skill, levels it up instead.
Owls are known for their wisdom, and they are ever ready to share it.
Travelling Circus
Levels up a random skill for each visiting hero.
Tricks, feats of acrobatics, and cheerful skits — which of these will you have time to see?
University
Allows each visiting hero to learn up to {0} random skills for {1} Gold each.
Many brilliant minds on Enroth hail from Jadamean schools.
Infernal Cirque
Allows each visiting hero to learn up to {0} random Expert skills or level them up.
Those who witness these performances are forever changed.
Redwood Observatory
Removes the fog of war in a huge radius around it.
It’s better than climbing a random tree!
Wind Rose
Fully reveals the whole map.
It allows one to see everything — not with lenses and spyglasses, but by charming winds.
Explorer’s Camp
Restores {0}% of hero’s maximum Movement points and {1}% maximum mana.
Sit down and rest your weary legs.
Stables
Restores {0}% of hero’s maximum Movement points and grants +{0}% until the end of the week.
Grooms at such waystations earn a tidy sum, especially in troubled times.
Fountain
Gives +{0} Movement points this turn.
Jadamean scholars confirm: horses drink.
Learning Stone
Gives +{0} XP to each visiting hero.
A timeless artifact, its knowledge passed down through generations, offering wisdom to those who seek it.
Town Gate
Teleports the visiting hero to any chosen city under your control.
Space is just another canvas for the mind.
Portal Exit
Someone can appear from this portal, but you cannot enter it.
There is no turning back.
Portal
Teleports the visiting hero to a paired portal.
It’s not that distance doesn’t exist; it’s just irrelevant.
Marketplace
Allows visiting heroes to exchange their resources for different ones.
The lack of competition emboldens merchants to hike up their prices.
Forge of the Second Man
Allows visiting heroes to trade resources for artifacts.
Forging is their form of prayer. They don’t much care for what happens with the results and will share them happily for a donation.
Sacrificial Shrine
Allows visiting heroes to sacrifice artifacts and creatures for experience.
It is better not to dwell on what entity rewards you for your blood sacrifices.
Chimerologist
Allows visiting heroes to trade creatures for other, random creatures.
Transmutation is an art in which science and chaos intertwine. It yields unpredictable, often surprising results.
Tavern
Allows visiting heroes to recruit additional heroes.
Even the weirdest of heroes need a drink and a chat sometimes.
Insara’s Eye
Gives the owner an additional level of knowledge about their opponents, as well as {0} Law points daily.
The godling of opportunities and progress keeps a keen eye on the best.
Hero Cage
Unlock to release a friendly hero.
How long have they been rotting in that cell?
Well
Fully restores each visiting hero’s mana.
Mana brims within this well, nearly spilling over the edge.
Pit of Glory
Allows visiting heroes to upgrade their creatures for Gold.
Independent veterans fighting here will happily teach your troops — for a price.
Goblin Cache
Once per game, grants a random small reward.
All the valuables in this cache are stolen. And stealing from a thief? That’s no crime.
Abandoned Cart
Once per game, grants a random small reward.
The crates in this cart have dried and cracked with age. Their owner is clearly not coming back for them.
Crow Nest
Once per game, grants a random small reward.
Jadamean crows carry all manner of trinkets back to their nests.
Forgotten Remains
Once per game, grants a random small reward.
Whoever killed this traveler clearly had no interest in robbery.
The Monty Hall
Once per game, offers a choice of {0} among {1} random artifacts.
They say the other two artifacts turn into goats afterwards.
Tomb of a Nameless Hero
Once per game, grants a random artifact. Curses its plunderer.
No god or godling approves of whoever just takes the offerings to a hero.
Raiders’ Camp
Gives Gold and possibly also valuable artifacts after defeating its guard.
They camp across Jadame, attacking random villages and travelers. No remorse.
Ancient Crypt
Gives Gold and possibly also valuable artifacts after defeating its guard.
This crypt is so old, no one remembers who was buried there. If there even was anyone.
Legion’s Memorial
Gives Gold, Wood, and Ore after defeating its guard.
Scattered across Jadame, these statues are often used as convenient landmarks and gathering points.
Alvar Outpost
Gives common and valuable resources after defeating its guard.
A safehouse used by the Triumvirate’s spies to rest between missions.
Prismatic Nest
Gives Gems, Crystals, and Mercury after defeating its guard.
The inhabitants are united by their love for colorful nesting.
Uncanny Rite
Gives Gold and Mercury after defeating its guard.
Whatever is happening here, it bodes ill and must be stopped.
Circle of Life
Gives Gold and Crystals after defeating its guard.
The woods and the Hive, united in their understanding that the dead are merely carrion to be recycled.
Iridescent Abbey
Gives Gold and Gems after defeating its guard.
Craftsmen use Gems for stained glass that some believe possesses miraculous qualities.
Troglodyte Throne
Gives a very large amount of Gold after defeating its guard.
They do say that luck is blind, after all.
Overgrown Vori Ruins
Gives a very large amount of Gold and resources after defeating its guard.
In the olden days, Vori elves created exquisite outposts all over Jadame. Now they are gone to their secluded land.
Twilight Bloom
Gives Gold and a valuable artifact after defeating its guard.
This mysterious plant grows as if springing directly from the elemental planes.
Dragon Utopia
Gives a very large amount of Gold and valuable artifacts after defeating its guard.
The utopian fantasy in question being that different dragons can live united and in peace.
Research Laboratory
Gives a very large amount of Gold and valuable artifacts after defeating its guard.
All the “research” here is of necromantic nature — including attempts to resurrect dragons. So far, they have not succeeded, but they’ve certainly accumulated a great deal of funds.
Black Tower
Gives a valuable artifact, some Gold, or resources after defeating its guard.
A towering, ominous structure looming in the distance, filled with dangerous creatures and hidden treasures.
Abandoned Mansion
Grants Gold and possibly also valuable artifacts after defeating its guard.
The rich are well‑supplied and eager to donate to your cause, after some persuasion.
Mearea’s Altar
Grants Gold after defeating its guard.
Mearea grants her golden blessing to the worthy alone.
Cursed Old House
Gives valuable artifacts after defeating its guard.
It was a building with a peaked roof, black with age and devoid of any charm… The house stood in silence, keeping the secrets of past years.
Abnormal Structure
Gives valuable artifacts after defeating its guard.
Among the mangled figments of the building, the Rift spawns prowl. What are they guarding?
Arborcopia
Once per game, triggers one week of creature growth in all cities under the player’s control.
Life! Life! Life bursts from the ground, unprompted!
Eternal Dragon
Allows visiting heroes to transform their dragons into very powerful Lich Dragons.
Dragons used to be large as a city, with three eyes that saw past, present, and future. We can only try to rebuild that power now.
Mirage
Defeat your better self to coalesce the illusion into a Grail. This treasure will allow the hero to build a unique and wondrous building in a city.
An artifact so ancient and powerful, it can take any shape... and be brilliant in it.
Hell Light Arena
The Arena of Champions! After it’s visited and a few days pass, a battle will occur. The winner of the battle wins the game. If the enemy does not appear, you win by default.
They say true duels of skill can only be won under Hell’s lights.
Mercenary Guild
Allows the hero to recruit up to {0} random units. Refreshes every week.
Look, not everyone has the privilege of having personal drama. Sometimes people simply fight for money.
Underground Labyrinth
Defeat its guards to obtain Basilisks. Guards and Basilisks respawn every week.
Essssssshthhasssaaa…
This dwelling has a limited amount of creatures available for recruitment, which does not refresh each week.
Garrison
You can recruit Swordsmen in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Fancy tactics might be useful, but they won’t achieve much without basic infantry.
Mews
You can recruit Crossbowmen in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Though crossbowmen eventually supplanted the old archer units, the hawk‑mews endured — a lingering ghost of a bygone era.
Griffin Rookery
You can recruit Griffins in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Yes, it’s incredibly fancy. Griffins are entitled to the best nests in the land.
Sundrop Chapel
You can recruit Lightweavers in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Pray to the Chalice, and it shall heal and bolster you. Or let professionals do the praying for you.
Hippodrome
You can recruit Cavalry in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Horse races are held regularly at the hippodromes, and occasionally even jousting tournaments.
Threshold Basilica
You can recruit Inquisitors in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Humans stand on the verge of becoming pure light here, but still retaining human forms and social roles.
Radiant Forge
You can recruit Angels in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The Church’s ill‑wishers like to say that true angels split from the templars. But if that’s the case, then whose armors are forged here?
Crypts and Graves
You can recruit Skeletons in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The simplest way to obtain unfractured skeletons is from a grave.
Quiet Pavilion
You can recruit Wights in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Brought back against their will, they are drawn by reflections — both physical and mental.
Kennel
You can recruit Undead Pets in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Dogs are so loyal to their masters that they often continue to serve nonchalantly even after passing.
Bone Exchange
You can recruit Graverobbers in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Necromancers are often too detached to gather bones themselves — and that’s where a savvy entrepreneur steps in!
Timeless Mansion
You can recruit Liches in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The undead aristocracy relentlessly pursues the study of death, and in doing so, they delve into the study of time itself.
Tomb of Warriors
You can recruit Dread Knights in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
War never ends — here, it lingers in the minds of those who perished.
Chateau of Feasts
You can recruit Vampires in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Eternal life brings eternal feasts and unending luxuries.
Faun Huts
You can recruit Fauns in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The fey folk make their home here, crafting their tools and weapons of war from the very fabric of the land.
Hop Patch
You can recruit Hoplets in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Seemingly cute to some, hoplets are fierce defenders of the Murmurwoods, and these humble homes stand as their stronghold.
Menhir Circle
You can recruit Vine Iriyads in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The trees and stones are alive here, occasionally wandering off of their own accord.
Blooming Pond
You can recruit Naiads in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Those who tend to the water will be rewarded by the creatures that dwell within.
Shroomwood Shack
You can recruit Herbomancers in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The most experienced ’mancers seclude themselves within these peculiar structures to commune with the Mycelium.
Thunder Lair
You can recruit Qilins in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The domains of air are tempestuous, and much tribute is required to tame the wind‑infused creatures that dwell above.
Pyre
You can recruit Phoenixes in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The flames burn not from wood, but from the collective life force of the forest itself.
Warren
You can recruit Troglodytes in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Pitch darkness reigns in the troglodyte burrows, making it impossible to navigate by sight.
Safe House
You can recruit Infiltrators in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
They stay for a night and then depart for a new mission.
Amphitheater
You can recruit Onyx Dancers in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
In times of peace, Dancers enjoy changing accessories, though some admirers prefer them to maintain a constant look.
Labyrinth
You can recruit Minotaurs in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Built by minotaurs, Balthazar’s Lair is arguably the most magnificent city in Jadame.
Stilled Voices
You can recruit Medusae in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Be respectful, or your voice will fall silent as well.
Chthonic Home
You can recruit Hydras in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The house serves as quarters for the hydra breeders, while the creatures themselves are kept in large underground pens.
Cave Palace
You can recruit Cave Dragons in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Even with a plethora of dragons living above ground, these ones prefer to stay in the shadows.
Lesser Summoning Rite
You can recruit Ra’Shoth in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Summoning can be tumultuous, but the chains hold it firmly in place.
Cultist Spire
You can recruit Cultists in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The rooms are ill‑suited for practicing rigorous self‑control.
Aga’Shoth Stables
You can recruit Aga’Shoth Riders in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Aga’Shoth sleep comfortably inside. The riders have their own space upstairs, but they often join their mounts.
Disturbing Summoning Rite
You can recruit Grand Shoth in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The center of the portal glows and shimmers with the colours from beyond this world.
House of Chains
You can recruit Concubi in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The windows here are tiny, for the inhabitants have abandoned all need for light.
Bloated Mansion
You can recruit Arbitrators in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Half‑drowned in Abyssal emanations, the mansion itself pulses with life.
Eerie Summoning Rite
You can recruit Abyssal Envoys in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
They will come when called.
Neglected Housing
You can recruit Parasites in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Parasites do not care much about the state of their hosts’ houses.
Carrion Lair
You can recruit Locusts in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The smell is unbearable.
Paper Nest
You can recruit Hornets in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
It should be noted that one of civilization’s greatest achievements, paper, was invented in a hornet nest — though its inhabitants never learned to write.
Chitinous Ziggurat
You can recruit Scorpions in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Made from the sturdy carapaces of its inhabitants.
Apex
You can recruit Reavers in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
Reavers would never settle for a lower building, no matter how practical it might be.
Burning Soul Burrows
You can recruit Waurmos in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
The waurmos’ tunnels stretch for many leagues in all directions.
Tower of Love
You can recruit Hive Queens in this dwelling. Their weekly growth increases by {0}.
“Come, darling, and witness Our Master’s gentle caress…”
Temple of the Four Scholars
You can recruit Sentinels of Glory in this dwelling.
Though what is Glory, and why do we need sentinels to guard it?
Dragon Crag
You can recruit Obsidian Dragons in this dwelling.
Only the mightiest champions earn allies here. The rest are merely food.
Watcher Platform
You can recruit Worldwatchers in this dwelling.
Why the worldwatchers allow themselves to be “recruited” remains unknown. Yet, it certainly has nothing to do with gold.
Pyramid
You can recruit Couatls in this dwelling.
These places are said to be the work of Hksmilla, where its mightiest servants now reside.
Abandoned Tower
You can recruit Manticores in this dwelling.
Manticores favor high places, nesting where they can spot prey from miles away.
Dragon Eggshells
You can recruit Faerie Dragons in this dwelling.
Faerie Dragons keep a touch of their childhood into adulthood, often shaping their homes to resemble the eggs they hatched from.
Blooming Marsh
You can recruit Giant Toads in this dwelling.
Places like these, rich in both life and mana, give rise to the strangest amphibians.
Unicorn Vale
You can recruit Unicorns in this dwelling.
Where creatures of pure beauty answer the call of a pure heart.
Dragonslayer Base
You can recruit Dragonslayers in this dwelling.
Seen by many as a little unhinged, Dragonslayers establish enclaves in remote locations, taking on apprentices with little to lose and everything to gain.
Crystal Nest
You can recruit Kittenhorns in this dwelling.
Adoring crystals to the point of fusing them into their bodies, it’s no surprise that these voracious kittens make their nests among them.
Starshard
You can recruit Starchildren in this dwelling.
Where does it come from? The beings you can recruit here will follow you, but they will never answer this question.
Sundew Thicket
You can recruit Primal Remnants in this dwelling.
A place as ancient as the world itself.
Battlefield
You can recruit Animated Armors in this dwelling.
Places of conflict can breed more than just undead… if the Mycelium reaches it first.
Terror Wood
You can recruit Grolls in this dwelling.
A place straight out of a fairytale. Monsters do dwell here.
Burrow
You can recruit Halflings in this dwelling.
Wartime forces even the most peaceful homebodies to go cross their own thresholds.
Village Hut
You can recruit Peasants in this dwelling.
They’ll join any army just to avoid paying taxes to their lord.
Pixie Tower
You can recruit Pixies in this dwelling.
The playful residents are eager to join any hero who promises them a good time.
Point of Balance
Defeat its guard to obtain Sentinels of Glory.
Sentinels of Glory are guardians of balance — unwavering in their resolve and cautious of the Church’s expanding influence.
Colosseum
Defeat its guard to obtain Cavalry.
Jousting and griffin fights! They’ll gladly follow you to a more open area for better jousting action.
Ritual Pyre
Defeat its guard to obtain Phoenixes.
Summoning Phoenixes is the most challenging of the elemental rituals. It requires great pain.
Tomb of Vigilance
Defeat its guard to obtain Dread Knights.
Their loyal Undead Pets refused to leave the grave, even after their masters’ death.
Boreal Call
Defeat its guard to obtain Abyssal Envoys.
Sometimes, the barrier between the Material World and the Realm Between Realms grows so thin that abominable horrors spill through the cracks.
Carrion Pile
Defeat its guard to obtain Waurmos.
The gigantic rotting body reeks unbearably and, more importantly, teems with scavengers.
Petrified Memorial
Defeat its guard to obtain Medusae.
A hall where medusae display their most immaculate statues, each one frozen in time — trophies of their deadly craft.
Remote Foothold
Allows heroes to leave creatures and artifacts here and immediately transfer them to another Remote Foothold you control.
It’s all about secret underground passages.
Pocket Dimension
Allows heroes to leave creatures and artifacts here and immediately transfer them to any Remote Foothold or Pocket Dimension you control.
With sound engineering and potent magic, anything is possible.
Abandoned Outpost
Defeat its guard to transform this outpost into a city of your starting faction.
It has all the basics needed for life… all it lacks is identity.
You can recruit Gnats in this dwelling.
Their monotonous humming is heard from afar.
Campaign Block
You cannot go through until this block is removed.
I am a campaign block.
Duration: unlimited.
Duration: until next week.
Duration: until next day.
Duration: until the end of next battle.
Duration: {0} round(s).
Duration: {0} week(s).
Duration: {0} day(s).
Duration: {0} battle(s).
+{0}% Movement points.
+{0}% maximum mana.
+{0} Morale.
+{0} Luck.
The hero can fly. Flying above obstacles costs +{0}% Movement points per step.

Broken Bridge
Until it’s repaired, there’s no way to cross.
Who broke it? And why?
Tether
A strange, otherworldly knot of mushrooms and roots.
Does it grow out of the ground — or into it?
Empty Mine
The bright red glow has faded from these crystals. Strange…
Useless rocks.
Ominous Altar
A deadly aura clings to this place.
Eerie.
Cage
Several Lightweavers are imprisoned within.
This can’t be good…
What is this madness?..
Are they… alive?
Construction Site
Something is being built here.
No wonder the feyfolk are so agitated.
Forgotten Diary
Reading it may shed some light on the mysterious traveler.
Though, honestly… shame on you for snooping.
Tower of the Stargazer
Home to the mad stargazer who speaks to the skies.
Then again… aren’t we all a little mad?
Abandoned Shack
Someone must have lived here a very long time ago
Not too bad — a bit of décor could give it new life.
Friends’ House
A place where two lost souls have finally found their peace.
Everything feels better with a dog around.
Cage
Valentina must be locked inside.
It’s disturbing that human‑sized cages are just lying around like this.
Mearea Shrine
An ancient altar carved with symbols of gold, light and luck.
Blessed be.
Necromancy Amplifier
An obelisk pulsating with dark energy.
An ancient technology long forgotten by many.
Destroyed Amplifier
Ruins stripped of all energy.
With each one lost, the undead army grows weaker.
Weird Hut
Fit for a mage or a scientist.
What secrets lie within…?
Forest Altar
An ancient, ominous structure; a haunting reminder of darker times.
Some say the shadows here still whisper forgotten secrets.
Pulsating Mushroom
It seems to pulse with a strange, otherworldly energy.
Touch it, and who knows what might happen…
Mycelium Remnants
If you stand close, you can almost hear faint murmurs.
Mycelium roots spread throughout Jadame — sometimes unseen, yet ever whispering.
Phoenix Egg
Something has halted the phoenix’s rebirth.
A silence where fire should rise.
Strange Monument
This statue feels oddly out of place, as if it doesn’t belong here.
You can’t shake the feeling that if you came on another day… you would have found something else instead.
